title: Power BI Security Dashboard Layout — KPI Cards, RAG Status, Matrix, Trend Charts, Drill-Through

confirmed_facts.fact: Recommended security dashboard page hierarchy: (1) Summary — KPI cards header, trend charts, metric matrix. (2) Monthly Detail — full matrix with MoM deltas, drill-through target. (3) Quarterly/YoY — quarterly comparison. (4) Metric Detail — drill-through for individual metrics with raw data table.

New Card visual (2023+) supports main measure plus reference labels and secondary values in a compact card. KPI visual shows value + sparkline + vs-target with auto-coloring. Set Direction = 'Low is good' for security metrics where decreasing counts are positive.

Conditional formatting icon sets: Format pane > Cell elements > Icons > Rules. Layout = 'Data only' shows icon without number. Use integer measures (0=Green, 1=Amber, 2=Red) mapped to circle/flag icons.
Drill-through: target page fields placed in Drill through field well (not Filters pane) define what context is passed. Power BI auto-generates a Back button on the detail page.

Power BI natively supports sparklines in Table and Matrix visuals (since 2022). Each row gets a small trend line. Configure Y-axis min/max in Format pane > Sparklines for consistent scaling across rows.

implementation_notes:
Summary page layout zones:
- Zone 1 (top): KPI cards — one per key metric (Sev5, Sev4, External, Internal, Alerts)
- Zone 2 (upper middle): Trend line chart — last 12 months, multiple metrics as series
- Zone 3 (lower middle): Multi-metric matrix — rows=metric, columns=last 6 months, RAG icons
- Zone 4 (bottom): Period slicer (dropdown) + metric filter slicer

KPI card with vs-prior-month comparison:
```dax
Sev5 Count Current = CALCULATE([VulnCount], MetricDim[MetricName] = "Sev5")

Sev5 vs PM =
    VAR _Current = [Sev5 Count Current]
    VAR _Prior   = CALCULATE([Sev5 Count Current], PREVIOUSMONTH('DateTable'[Date]))
    VAR _Chg     = IF(ISBLANK(_Prior) || _Prior = 0, BLANK(), DIVIDE(_Current - _Prior, ABS(_Prior)))
    RETURN
        IF(ISBLANK(_Chg), "No prior",
        IF(_Chg > 0, "▲ " & FORMAT(ABS(_Chg), "0.0%") & " vs PM",
                     "▼ " & FORMAT(ABS(_Chg), "0.0%") & " vs PM"))

// Card background color based on value
Sev5 Card Color =
    VAR _Val = [Sev5 Count Current]
    RETURN IF(ISBLANK(_Val), "#FFFFFF",
           IF(_Val >= 100, "#FF4444",
           IF(_Val >= 50,  "#FFC000", "#70AD47")))
// Format pane > Background > Conditional formatting > Field value > [Sev5 Card Color]
```

Dynamic RAG with per-metric thresholds from MetricDim:
```dax
RAG Status =
    VAR _Val   = [Metric Value]
    VAR _Red   = SELECTEDVALUE(MetricDim[RedThreshold], 100)
    VAR _Amber = SELECTEDVALUE(MetricDim[AmberThreshold], 50)
    RETURN IF(ISBLANK(_Val), BLANK(),
           SWITCH(TRUE(), _Val >= _Red, 2, _Val >= _Amber, 1, 0))
// SELECTEDVALUE works because matrix row context filters MetricDim to one metric
// Use with icon conditional formatting: 2=Red circle, 1=Amber circle, 0=Green circle
```

Normalised trend measure for multi-metric comparison:
```dax
// When metrics have different scales (Sev4=800, Alerts=5)
Metric Normalised =
    VAR _Val = [Metric Value]
    VAR _Max = CALCULATE(MAX(SecuritySnapshots[Value]), ALL('DateTable'), ALLSELECTED(MetricDim))
    RETURN IF(_Max = 0, BLANK(), DIVIDE(_Val, _Max) * 100)
// Or use Combo chart: column Y axis for high-volume, line Y axis for low-volume
```

Dynamic dashboard title and context:
```dax
Dashboard Title =
    VAR _Min = MIN('DateTable'[MonthYear])
    VAR _Max = MAX('DateTable'[MonthYear])
    RETURN "Security KPI Dashboard — " & _Min & " to " & _Max

Last Refresh = FORMAT(NOW(), "DD MMM YYYY HH:MM")

Data As Of = FORMAT(MAX('DateTable'[Date]), "MMMM YYYY")
```

Drill-through setup:
1. Create 'Detail' report page
2. Drag MetricDim[MetricName] and DateTable[MonthYear] to Drill through field well
3. Add table visual with individual records filtered by drilled context
4. Power BI auto-adds Back button (if deleted, re-add with Action > Type = Back)
5. On Summary page matrix: right-click cell > Drill through > Detail

Note: drill-through fields go in the Drill through field well, NOT the Filters pane — they look similar but are different.

Sparklines in matrix rows:
1. Select Table/Matrix visual > Visualizations pane > Add sparkline (chart icon in Values)
2. Select measure ([VulnCount]) and axis (DateTable[MonthYear])
3. Format pane > Sparklines > Y-axis > Range: set fixed min=0, max=500 for consistent scaling
4. Typical layout: Metric | Latest Count | MoM% | Trend (sparkline)
caveats: KPI cards without comparison values are useless — always add vs-prior-month or vs-threshold for context
Same threshold for all metrics (e.g. red >= 100) doesn't work — build per-metric thresholds into MetricDim
Trend chart with all metrics on same Y-axis is unusable when scales differ — use field parameter slicer or normalise
MetricDim[SortOrder] Sort by Column must be set — otherwise metric rows appear alphabetically instead of by priority
KPI visual with 'Low is good' not set for vulnerability counts celebrates high values as green
Drill-through pages without a Back button leave users stranded — always include one
Cards showing BLANK for no selection — add COALESCE or IF(ISBLANK) to show 0 or a message
